Glen Canyon, Greenehaven underground transmission line permit (AZ,UT) : environmental impact statement.

The proposal involves granting a permit for an underground power transmission line to serve the Greenehaven Development near Glen Canyon National Recreation Area, Arizona. This line was installed without prior authorization, and the permit would legitimize its presence. The development aims to provide residential and commercial facilities, potentially increasing local population and economic activity. However, it raises concerns about environmental impacts and the strain on local resources, particularly water, which is already allocated for other uses. The community is divided, with some supporting the development for economic reasons, while others oppose it due to environmental concerns.

The Glen Canyon underground transmission line was proposed to support the Greenehaven Development, which aimed to enhance local residential and commercial facilities near the Glen Canyon National Recreation Area. The line was installed without proper authorization, prompting the need for a permit to legitimize its existence. The project faced significant public pushback due to concerns about environmental impacts and resource allocation, particularly regarding water use in an already strained area. Ultimately, the community remains divided on the project's benefits versus its environmental costs.

"The Sierra Club is opposed to the continued development of the second home subdivision and trailer park known as Greenehaven. The legislative history of the Act establishing Glen Canyon National Recreation Area is very clear. Congress did not believe that such developments were appropriate or needed within the boundaries of the N.R.A." — John A. McComb, Southwest Representative, Sierra Club

"The publicly stated policy of the Arizona State Land Dept. is to use the state trust lands for the highest monetary gain and not necessarily the best use. This attitude has led and will continue to lead to other Greenehavens." — William F. Briney, President, Prescott Chapter, Izaak Walton League of America
